title: Unfair Coin (Bayes)

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-04-probability-theory.md

setup.narrative: Among 1000 coins, exactly ONE has heads on both sides; the other 999 are fair. You pick one coin uniformly at random and flip it 10 times. All 10 tosses come up heads.
rules: Coins are picked uniformly at random.
Tosses are independent (given the coin chosen).
Fair coin: P(heads) = 1/2 per toss. Two-headed coin: P(heads) = 1 per toss.
setup.question: Given the 10 heads observation, what is the probability you picked the two-headed coin?

learningObjectives: Set up Bayes' formula with prior P(unfair) = 1/1000.
Compute the likelihood ratio P(10 heads | unfair) / P(10 heads | fair).
Combine to get the posterior probability.

stages.label: Set up Bayes
stages.prompt: Let A = 'picked the unfair coin', B = '10 heads in 10 tosses'. Bayes: P(A|B) = P(B|A) P(A) / P(B). What are P(A), P(B|A), P(B|not A)?

options.text: P(A) = 1/1000.  P(B|A) = 1 (unfair coin always heads).  P(B|not A) = (1/2)^10 = 1/1024.

stages.explanation: Prior: 1 unfair out of 1000. Likelihoods: unfair always shows heads, fair shows heads with probability (1/2)^10.

stages.label: Compute P(A | B)
stages.explanation: 1024 / 2023 ≈ 0.5062. Despite the prior being only 1/1000 (very rare coin), 10 consecutive heads shifts the posterior to just over 1/2.

stages.label: Commit
stages.prompt: Given 10 consecutive heads, what is P(unfair coin)?

options.text: 100% (the unfair coin is the only one that always gives heads).

stages.explanation: About 50.6%. The likelihood ratio of 1024 (1024 times more likely under the unfair-coin hypothesis) almost exactly cancels the prior odds of 1:999 -- yielding posterior odds near 1:1.

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: How many heads in a row would shift the posterior to 95%?
What if there were 1 unfair coin among 10000? Recompute.
Express the answer as posterior ODDS rather than probability. (Posterior odds = prior odds * likelihood ratio.)
